author: JayWalk
content: Genuine question here for the very few people still around here that actually believe in this project.  What will it take before you stop blindly believing and realize something just doesn't add up?  The reason I ask is based in part to yet another answer in the Foundation Room where the Devs claim that they have something, but either can't talk about it or can't show it.  Doesn't that seem just a LITTLE suspect given that has been a common theme to most answers?  What will be your tipping point? SCREAM not coming out this year?  SCREAM looking like it did in that horrible clip?  What will it be?  And this has nothing to do with price action because I know it is just the market we are in.  It really comes down to the repeated we have it, but can't show it answers.

author: Eoganacht | BULK [BULK]
content:
Most AAA game development happens in complete secrecy. Sperasoft has in their contract that nothing can be shown before its finalized -- this is because for most of game development the assets etc look like crap to put it bluntly. It's only in the last 10% or so that the quality really comes through. 

Despite this they have shown us so much in Foundation Room that makes it clear the team is developing, that is not in question for me whatsoever. 

I imagine most of the work SA team has done this year is code, which they can't really share with us. 

When the team has been clear this entire time this will take 5-7 years, I am nowhere near the point where I'd tell the team to put up or shut up with under a year with UE5 so far.

Just my two ATLAS, to @[IA] nallgaier | BULK's point, I'd would also be interested in hearing what you find sketch

author: odvb
content: In general, I like the idea... both of a f2p version of some kind for the broader public to try (though I'm not sure about this...), and also linking it to a "lending" system to allow the public to use ships owned by the more committed community.  But I think there might be a simpler, tighter way to implement... by linking SCORE to f2p SCREAM.  Idea: all ships in SCORE create a pool from which f2p players can draw.  An f2p player picks a ship (or ships) from this pool with which to play, and then rewards they earn are spread equally to stakers of that ship-type (weighted by number each has staked of course).  The only incentive for f2p is the fun of the game, but you also wouldn't even need a wallet to play this version.  Could just jump on and play.  If thought through well, I think this could present an alternative to just phasing out SCORE: letting SCORE gradually turn into a simple lending system for ships in the game... like a ship-rental co-op basically.  The idea would be to slowly shift all ATLAS emmissions just to SCREAM, but then the SCREAM emissions earned through lending become the the rewards for SCORE.  Crazy first pass at this idea... thoughts anyone?  Could be crap, I dunno!

author: ! ᚺEIM | VɪᴋɪɴɢᴇɴBᴇɴɴʏViking
content:
Pretty much everything does lol. Question is how patient is the investors. Some of the really early ones can sell out now and still be in hefty profits. If we do not get gamers or atleast have a plan for integrating gamers in this crypto gaming space, it wont look good. I know very well we cannot do mass adoption untill actual gameplay. But publicly SA team is releasing new ships, delayed and put pretty much everything planned early this year on hold or delayed, playerbase and assets value has dropped down hefty (ya ya crypto down). Now i do fully understand the reasons for this and i love and support Star Atlas.

But everything that the average gamer or new investor sees publicly by looking at Star Atlas is a big red scam flag, it seems like a moneygrab before the ultimate rug. If we do not even try to improve this prior to the releases, it'll still look pretty little credible from a AAA title. Everything is seemingly going to shit and all the content thats being pushed out is new ships, its not a good sign to Gamers thats sceptical to the Crypto gaming space at all. All they hear and see for months is shit load of SA is a scam, on twitter, YT and every platform they are on. When people have heard about this for months, its hard to turn them around regardless of the web game.

author: [AEP] Dez [AEP]
content:
@𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul @Kr1gs 
Stakeholder - In business is used to describe any entity/person directly affected by your decisions. 

The specific line you are talking about is talking about Internal Development and Testing. So they are just saying that the teams connected to SCREAM have all been testing or playing with it and will report their opinions/suggestions/hard-changes needed for their parts to work or be better. 

So if we are talking about SCREAM, The Map/Navigation team may ask for something but if they changed it, the Mining team would have to change their mechanic, and so the 3D Artists need to rethink the design of the equipment. 

Stakeholders are an inclusive way of thinking of your whole process and effects to peers and sister-departments.

author: [AEP] Funcracker [AEP]
content:
@odvb I just read back a bit and thought to shed some light on the matter 

Sperasoft is a sub-contractor for Star Atlas. They are currently busy wrapping up the Showroom, and afterwards they will start on the next big module that is part of project “Rosegarden”.

The team shared earlier that they would like to initiate similar “partnerships”/arrangements with other sub-contractors, who would each work on a specific part of the UE5 experience.

SA is not like a publisher, almost all AAA games (particularly franchises) are partially made by companies such as Sperasoft, as evidenced by checking even just their website 

That other famous game, Star Citizen is doing exactly the same btw  They have several studios working on aspects of the their game as well.

This is a good thing as SA started out with 0 UE developers and these companies  already had a team of experienced developers that could start right away. It saves a lot of time being able to just go with an experienced team that has a solid working setup in place already 

Adding more in the mix would be good, as it would speed up UE5 development a lot. 

It is unclear to me if SA is looking to set up its own UE5 development team, though I would assume so. They will probably pick up small tidbits, until they have grown big enough to take on a module of their own.

But that last bit is just speculation from my side  Would be a good question perhaps for the team

author: odvb
content: Aug/Sept I believe  But they started with only a few people, and with discussing what the team wanted from them. They scaled up over time. And trust me there has been a lot of back and forth in the early stages, they needed to really get to know each other, the project and what the team wanted. These things take time.  Now that they have successfully gone through all of that, and Sperasoft is up to speed on what the team wants, and how the team works and communicates, the team feels more confident to let them work on a huge chunk of core gameplay with the ONI space station.  Other potential contractors will first work on “smaller”, more isolated modules, before potentially in the future able to also work on bigger, more important pieces

author: odvb
content: The Star Atlas is the actual full 3D map of the known galaxy. Originally, upon its launch is also when we would see coordinates being added to ships and assets, meaning you can no longer freely move them between players or accounts and deploy them wherever you want.   It was supposed to be used in both the mini-game and UE5, though my expectations was that they would initially launch it as a WebGL website for us, just to look at.  However, I am not sure where all of that stands, now that we’ve seen SCREAM is already incorporating a 3D galaxy map (or parts thereof at least)   It’s a good question

author: [AEP] Dez [AEP]
content:
Hanger Unlocks
All players will start SCREAM with some number of hangar spaces. There will be two mechanisms to increase your capacity. Upgrades and Unlocks. 
Upgrades- Using Atlas, players will be able to pay in order to upgrade the size of a hanger. This will allow them to house larger ships. 
Unlock- Players will also be able to unlock additional hanger bays, but only through gameplay progression.

author: 𝐑𝐎𝐌𝐄 | Virtuwul [ROME]
content: @[AEP] Bodhitree Time played question: this was answered in the Twitter spaces.  Chyp said that there is no cap.  Your share of the pie = (Your time played + in-game decisions + fleet size)/ the rest of the players score X total faction pie.  To keep it simple: if you played twice as much as the average player in the faction, you would earn twice as much.  Fleet size and strategic decisions will create bigger differentials.

author: Xap✨ [STAR]
content: @[AEP] Bodhitree Time played question: this was answered in the Twitter spaces.  Chyp said that there is no cap.  Your share of the pie = (Your time played + in-game decisions + fleet size)/ the rest of the players score X total faction pie.  To keep it simple: if you played twice as much as the average player in the faction, you would earn twice as much.  Fleet size and strategic decisions will create bigger differentials.

content:
⁠archived-foundation-room⁠
They addressed this in the Twitter Spaces. There are no diminishing returns for length of time played. It's more like there's a set glass of emissions/rewards and the more hours are put in the more the same amount gets distributed, so the trick is to put in more time compared to other players.

I’m up 30% to date on all of my SA investments, and I wish everyone else was too.

author: Kr1gs
content: I’ll say this much (and I’ve said it before).  The fact that the same guy who was saying that the price was disconnected from the fundamentals a few months ago on POLIS… is now here saying  that the fundamentals are insanely good at this valuation and he’s bought a ton, says a lot.  I can only really speak to POLIS since most of my analysis is based around it being the closest thing the project has to direct equity.  That being said, I’ve started looking at ships as well which also says a lot.  Usually me showing up and getting on my soap box for how undervalued something is, is a great sign for that asset.  What I see with POLIS at 73 cents is a fully diluted market cap for this project of 263 million dollars.  I think there is a chance that the funding of the project is somewhere in that ballpark.  If that’s the case, at these levels you are buying into a massive and ambitious project backed by an army of talented people with real fundamentals… for the valuation of the cash in the vault.  That literally never happens.  That’s VC level valuations or lower.  The closest comparable projects all have fully diluted market caps (even in this market) of 2.3 to 4 billion dollars (and they won’t be anywhere near as big as this if we deliver).  That gives a peer to peer comparison for SA of a potential 9x-16x from here with no improvement in the broader market.  If the broader market doubles from here, that could be an 18x-32x return.  And that’s the base case.  All pretty darn conservative.  We’ll see, I think I’ve got a home run here.

author: Signal
content: There’s like 2 billion gamers out there and almost none of them have ever played a crypto game, so clearly they are all very happy with a very high degree of centralization on asset ownership.  For those types of players (the majority) SA could just have escrow accounts or even just rent assets for subscription fees etc.  Completely frees up gamers who just want to play the game to not have to understand crypto.

author: odvb
content: Yeah ease of account creation is huge.  If I could just hop on my PC and link a credit card that results in a wallet being auto generated, that would completely remove one of the biggest barriers to entry.  Or even some kind of escrow system where SA holds assets in trust on their side and you get something on your side like a receipt of ownership etc.  Then you could buy assets and play and never have to understand crypto at all because that side of it is optionally manageable by SA itself.  Maybe not everybody cares about direct wallet ownership and it’s enough for them to get a certificate or online account points or something they can exchange for cash or assets later.  Maybe have dual paths or multiple paths of what account creation and ownership can look like.

reply.preview: Yeah ease of account creation is huge. If I could just hop on my PC and link a credit card that results in a wallet being auto generated, that would completely remove one of the biggest barriers to entry. Or even some kind of escrow system where SA holds assets in trust on their side and you get something on your side like a receipt of ownership etc. Then you could buy assets and play and never have to understand crypto at all because that side of it is optionally manageable by SA itself. Maybe not everybody cares about direct wallet ownership and it’s enough for them to get a certificate or online account points or something they can exchange for cash or assets later. Maybe have dual paths or multiple paths of what account creation and ownership can look like.

author: Lysimach | Make Polis Great [STAR]
content: I think it may be a good idea to divide battle into turns, with some degree of randomness added (like chance to evade attack), with each turn ended with an action from both sides being accepted, and each turn giving chance to flee (well, more a possibility to try to flee, it should also have a chance to success). This way all sorts of actions, stance switching could be possible. Take a look at Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire ship battling, i think it is a good example. And also fits well into our current blockchain tech.

reply.preview: I think it may be a good idea to divide battle into turns, with some degree of randomness added (like chance to evade attack), with each turn ended with an action from both sides being accepted, and each turn giving chance to flee (well, more a possibility to try to flee, it should also have a chance to success). This way all sorts of actions, stance switching could be possible. Take a look at Pillars of Eternity II: Deadfire ship battling, i think it is a good example. And also fits well into our current blockchain tech.

author: Signal
content: The balance has always been that ATMTA may some day no longer control this universe, not that it will some day cease to control itself.  But with a big and successful enough universe and enough POLIS, I’m sure that ATMTA will entertain a gigantic buyout from their own gigantic universe.

author: Signal
content: I just don’t know why you’d want that.  I want the founders and team tied to this thing forever.  They are the originators and soul of the project.  Talking about buying them out or them not being here when there isn’t even a universe yet is crazy.  Wanting to be able to trade pieces of their company while they try to build this thing is crazy.

author: Signal
content: POLIS is a stake in the SA universe.  I’m amped about that.

author: Signal
content: That’s absolutely enough.

author: [AEP] Dez [AEP]
content: All I am saying is that they are double-dipping IMO. They own 50% of the DAO, which ties them to the project, but they also own all of the IP, which is arguably more important/valuable that the project.

author: [AEP] Dez [AEP]
content: Would you hire a Dev to build you an app, but say he gets to own the source code?

author: Signal
content: We didn’t hire them we invested in them.  Bit different.  Also we are putting the cart way before the horse and have no idea how the company’s or founders stances will change with time.  It could all be a moot debate.

author: Signal
content: If I was in the middle of building something and you started coming at me about IP, my stance would likely be very different if what I built was very successful and I was already very wealthy from it and we are 20 years in the future talking about community and autonomy etc.

author: Signal
content: The ideal environment here right now is the SA team building an awesome thing in partnership with us and not anybody drawing lines about who is getting screwed etc.  We have a great relationship and situation right now.  This type of dialog can make it seem like us and them, and the moment the team decides that they are separate from us we’ve all already lost.

author: Signal
content: Either way, all I’m saying is “it’s early”.

author: Signal
content: I get your points and concerns.  I simply think your questions can have different answers at different points in time for what this all looks like, and at this precise moment in time, you couldn’t possibly expect the kind of answer you are looking for.

author: Signal
content: Maybe you can later.  But we have to all get to later together first.

author: [AEP] Dez [AEP]
content:
It's all novel, and I get that. And maybe that's where I am getting lost. The initial investments and even current ship sales flow to ATMTA, but Polis is what they pitch as the power. 

I'm just asking questions and debating. I make no assumptions that I am right. Just curious more than anything. It seems weird. 

I love this project for the reason that we can ask these questions, and for the fact that they answer us. I'm accumulating, even still. 

I think that I can do both, critique and champion the project.

author: Signal
content: I agree fully.  And anyone who knows my history knows I’m very vocal whether good, bad, or indifferent so we are kindred spirits just sharing two view points.  I’d point out that future sales and activities are all meant to add to the POLIS DAO,  but the initial sales were needed to build the treasury for the project itself.  I’m glad ATMTA has an awesome war chest for development, and also glad that I’ll share in the revenues of the future project and universe that they build.  Especially at these prices, I think we have a really good deal.  Much respect brother I appreciate the exchange.

author: DominicVainMod
content:
So my thinking for requirements is this:

1. Existing “Marvelous Creatures” get grandfathered into new foundation room
2. DACs can send 2-5 people who aren’t already in
3. Some amount of money in ships, polis, atlas, etc. gets you in
4. Once you hit a certain level in discord, you get in. The level would take time to get to ensure only active members get in this way.

I think these requirements would keep it pretty attainable while still providing some exclusivity to only people actually engaged with the community.

I haven’t officially proposed this or anything but this seems like a good way to do this to me. Some, or a lot, of people might not agree.

author: [AEP] Bodhitree
content: I like #1, #2 would be hard to quantify what qualifies as a dac and would result in people just creating a fake dac to get in or having a legit dac and getting overlooked. #3 tvl works too #4 discord lvl works ok but the ⁠💯┃count-with-us gives a serious misrepresentation of activity

author: odvb
content: I can answer this. A big focus of Santi and I’s is currently getting the discord ready for when the inevitable masses come.  That means deleting channels that aren’t used or the use case of that channel is used somewhere else. That also means adding channels that make sense and should 100% be in the discord.   The suggestion channel for example was deleted because, honestly, we take suggestions from everyone and everywhere all the time so it didn’t really have a purpose and wasn’t really used often.  On the other side, channels I’m looking to add are faction specific chats, a more thought out DAC category, updated FAQs and a “channel info” channel that goes over what each channel is used for.

author: Signal
content: All that really matters in my valuation of POLIS is net funding of the project to date in USD.  For now, we are betting on a project and paying a price for equity in the final product.  The valuation at this stage is really made up of only three things.  1.) How well funded is the project right now today?  2.) How likely is the project to be realized?  3.) If realized, what would the value of the project be?

author: [AEP] Moustacheride
content:
@Santi let’s play fill in the blank:
      (Blank) Of the galaxy. Movie

author: Signal
content: From there, you can take cash on hand, likelihood of success, and what success would be worth, to come up with project equity value.

author: Signal
content: Revenue won’t be a gauge for a very very long time.  But at least it’s revenue generating at all.

author: Signal
content: If the project is funded at a level of 200 million USD (seems possible from the GAO during the crypto mania NFT boom off of ships sold and prices), and you gave the project a 1.5x multiple to cash on hand for their odds of success and ability to create value (practically no premium at all to cash in the vault), you’d get a reasonable equity value at this super early no where near complete stage of 300 million.  That’s higher than current POLIS by market cap.

author: Signal
content: fundamentally disagree.  I don't know how funding level of a project has any bearing on its value.  POLIS is a cash-flow earning asset.  Value is based on expected value of future cash flows and that's basically it.  Modeling it that way is hard right now

author: Signal
content: Compared to other crypto game market caps, a normal expected value for just any old decent crypto game / metaverse play would be 2-4 billion even in this dumpster market.

author: Christian Smith
content: @[AEP] Boen Just  to brief you on the runway discussion, Ill brush over what I asked and what I was told since I was involved in that discussion. I essentially asked Wagner what currencies runway was being kept in and if there are enough funds in USDC or USD to support SA for years to come without need for additional funding. Because of security issues and risk management, he did not want to be overly specific and cited the Luna incident since its possible that there was a mass intentional attack in that instance. But what he did divulge is that yes they do have the necessary runway, all ATLAS earned from ship sales has been kept in ATLAS however, but this is the save for the ship sales in USDC. So while ATLAS may be taking a hit right now, they still have significant reserves which the team is confident will be sufficient for supporting them through a bear market.

author: [AEP] Boen
content: @[AEP] Boen Just  to brief you on the runway discussion, Ill brush over what I asked and what I was told since I was involved in that discussion. I essentially asked Wagner what currencies runway was being kept in and if there are enough funds in USDC or USD to support SA for years to come without need for additional funding. Because of security issues and risk management, he did not want to be overly specific and cited the Luna incident since its possible that there was a mass intentional attack in that instance. But what he did divulge is that yes they do have the necessary runway, all ATLAS earned from ship sales has been kept in ATLAS however, but this is the save for the ship sales in USDC. So while ATLAS may be taking a hit right now, they still have significant reserves which the team is confident will be sufficient for supporting them through a bear market.

author: [IA] Conparator | BULKMUD
content:
AFAIK the TOS is exactly what it is called, the terms under which the service is provided.
IIRC the TOS (like the "AGB" in Germany) have to align with the laws which are enforced in the country of the main-headquarters of the company.
Means, if you are customer of a service offered by a company located in e.g. England, the TOS have to align with the laws of England.
The TOS are not laws themselves, they are the terms of the company under which they offer the service.
Means, as long as the TOS are not in conflict with the law of the company's location, they can further restrict the terms and conditions for their offered services.
If now a customer breaks the TOS but not a law, the company takes action against the customer (no legal advise, that's only what I've heard and read in the past). The actions against the customer would be at highest level e.g. service-suspension AFAIK.
If the customer not only breaks the TOS but also laws (either the ones of the country of the company's location or the country the customer is located), the related country takes action against the customer.
AFAIK this is why you e.g. are breaking Germany's laws when you (try) to purchase illegal goods (illegal in Germany) via a webstore of a company which is located in a country in which the good is legal.
The company isn't allowed at all to sell you as German customer such goods, so you are also breaking their TOS. Additionally you break the German law by purchasing illegal goods.

This is no legal advise. It's just what I've heard, read and interpreted

content: The team has clarified they are designing hard gameplay benefits to human operation of crew roles, including of course manual piloting. author: ! ᚺEIM | VɪᴋɪɴɢᴇɴBᴇɴɴʏViking
content: The only advantage I can see is that from day one they have a fully developed engine in UE5 that the SC crew never had the luxury of.  They had to build an engine from scratch to get where they are now, and they are stuck continuing to build and work with their homemade engine to progress further.  SA should be able develop much faster given equivalent resources.  Starting with UE5 out of the gate as a fully developed engine will be a big advantage.  They might actually beat Star Citizen to completion of their own idea.

author: Eoganacht | BULK [BULK]
content: I think you are going the right direction. I'm working in that kind of field and for the gambling industries in Germany e.g.It's the responsibility of the operator/business to spot gambling issues to protect the customer/player from himself  and to ruin his/her life including a suicide Hotline and to inform authorities. Worst case scenario is that the player will be excluded from every game/gambling all over Germany by listed in a database which is synchronized throughout the industries every 24h. You can browse for more details in Google and find Oasis In Darmstadt which is the responsible Government unit in handling this cases. I find this a great topic and appreciate the effort of the German government to protect gambling addicted people. This is applicable for other countries as well depending on your company sector listing. How this is applicable for online games on Metaverse/Blockchain I believe this needs to be crafted and probably it will come if governments become aware. We need to block access to this accounts including IP blocks and restrictions. The IT system does this automatically and spots certain fraud by player. Same for KYC.

author: Support Line | Star Atlas Mod [STAR]
content: There's a lot we still need to figure out when it comes to how involved we become with DACs. We're going to have extensive discussions about this very topic when people come back next week.  As for the IEC DAC. We gathered a statement from him and asked for him to provide proof of his statement. With that being said, what he showed proved his case to the community team. While we don't think his goal was to steal his DACs money and run, we can see why they thought that when he was M.I.A for months. Going forward with this situation, we'll most likely make and pin an official message in the guild channels with our findings so people can make their own decision on the situation.  I would really like to take this moment in time to express the necessity to DO YOUR OWN RESEARCH when joining or participating in anything involving money.   Even joining a DAC in our community carries risk if they have some sort of token, DAO or anything else that requires your money/ships/POLIS.  If there's anything I'm missing or you have have any questions, lmk friend.
